fields.name: Journal Template Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the journal template, the basis of the journal batch, that the entries were posted from.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the journal line.

fields.name: Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item on the journal line.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the posting date for the entry.

fields.name: Entry Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of transaction that will be posted from the item journal line.

fields.name: Source No.
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a document number for the journal line.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the item on the journal line.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the inventory location where the item on the journal line will be registered.

fields.name: Inventory Posting Group
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Source Posting Group
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of units of the item to be included on the journal line.

fields.name: Invoiced Quantity
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Unit Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the price of one unit of the item on the journal line.

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line's net amount.

fields.name: Discount Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the discount amount of this entry on the line.

fields.name: Salespers./Purch. Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the salesperson or purchaser who is linked to the sale or purchase on the journal line.

fields.name: Source Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the source code that has been entered on the item journal template.
The source code is automatically inserted on all journals that are created under the journal template.

fields.name: Applies-to Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the quantity on the journal line must be applied to an already-posted entry. In that case, enter the entry number that the quantity will be applied to.

fields.name: Item Shpt. Entry No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Indirect Cost %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of the item's last purchase cost that includes indirect costs, such as freight that is associated with the purchase of the item.

fields.name: Source Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the table number of the table that is the source of the journal line.

fields.name: Journal Batch Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the journal batch, a personalized journal layout, that the entries were posted from.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reason code, a supplementary source code that enables you to trace the entry.

fields.name: Recurring Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies a recurring method, if you have indicated that the journal is recurring.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the expiration date, if any, of the item carrying the item tracking number.

fields.name: Recurring Frequency
fields.tooltip: Specifies a recurring frequency if it is indicated that the journal is recurring.

fields.name: Drop Shipment
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Transaction Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of transaction that the document represents, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Transport Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the transport method, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region of the address.

fields.name: New Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the new location to link the items on this journal line.

fields.name: New Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the new dimension value code that will link to the items on the journal line.

fields.name: New Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the new dimension value code that will link to the items on the journal line.

fields.name: Qty. (Calculated)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity on hand of the item.

fields.name: Qty. (Phys. Inventory)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity on hand of the item as determined from a physical count.

fields.name: Last Item Ledger Entry No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the item ledger entry that was used to calculate the quantity on hand.
The field is used when you use the feature Physical Inventory to insert items in the journal.

fields.name: Phys. Inventory
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether the journal line was created from the Phys. Inventory Journal.
A check mark in the field indicates that the line was created from this journal line.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's or customer's trade type to link transactions made for this business partner with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Entry/Exit Point
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code of either the port of entry at which the items passed into your country/region or the port of exit through which you shipped the items out of your country/region.
The code is used for reporting to INTRASTAT and will appear on the Intrastat Journal.
To see the entry/exit points that you can choose, choose the field.

fields.name: Document Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the related document was created.

fields.name: External Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a document number that refers to the customer's or vendor's numbering system.

fields.name: Area
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for an area of the customer or vendor with which you are trading the items on this journal line.
The code is used for reporting to INTRASTAT and will appear on the Intrastat Journal.
To see the area codes that you can choose in the Area table, choose the field.

fields.name: Transaction Specification
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a code for the transaction specification that should be used in this journal line. To see the transaction specification codes that have been set up in the Transaction Specification table choose the field.
The code will be used in INTRASTAT reporting.

fields.name: Posting No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Contains the code for the number series that will be used to assign a document number to the journal line when it is posted. To see the number series that have been set up in the No. Series table, choose the field.
The program automatically copied the number series code from the Posting No. Series field on the journal batch. You can change the code.
If this field is blank when you post the line, the document number in the Document No. field will also be used on the posted line.

fields.name: Reserved Quantity
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Unit Cost (ACY)
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Source Currency Code
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the transfer of the document type from documents being posted, such as purchase orders and sales orders, to the posted entries.

fields.name: Document Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the transfer of the document line number from documents being posted, such as purchase orders and sales orders, to the posted entries

fields.name: Order Type
fields.tooltip:
Shows which type of order that the entry was created in. You can select from the following options:

<Blank> when the entry comes from any other transaction than the following options

Production

Transfer

Service

Assembly

fields.name: Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the order that created the entry.

fields.name: Order Line No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the line number of the order that created the entry.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: New Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Contains a reference to a combination of new dimension values.
The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.

fields.name: Assemble to Order
fields.tooltip:
This field is used internally.

Specifies if the item journal line is used to post an assemble-to-order sale.
The item ledger entry that results from posting an assemble-to-order sale is fixed applied to the related item ledger entry for the assembly output. Accordingly, the cost of an assemble-to-order sale is derived from the assembly order that it was linked to.

fields.name: Job No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the job that is associated with the entry.

fields.name: Job Task No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the job task that is associated with the entry.

fields.name: Job Purchase
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the entry relates to a purchase for a job.

fields.name: Job Contract Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the entry number of the job planning line that the item journal line is linked to.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the variant of the item on the line.

fields.name: Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a bin code for the item.

fields.name: Qty. per Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code if you have filled in the Sales Unit of Measure field on the item card and entered a quantity for the current item in the Qty. of Base Unit of Measure per Unit field.

fields.name: New Bin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the new bin code to link to the items on this journal line.

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

fields.name: Derived from Blanket Order
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if the line is related to a blanket sales order. When you post the journal, the program transfers the information to the Item Ledger Entry table.
The program fills in this field automatically, and it is not editable.

fields.name: Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The field shows the Quantity field on the line expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Invoiced Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: This field is automatically updated. The field shows the Invoiced Quantity field on the line expressed in base units of measure.

fields.name: Reserved Qty. (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the item reserved for the line.

fields.name: Level
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Manages line indentation in the Description field.

fields.name: Flushing Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies how consumption of the item (component) is calculated and handled in production processes. Manual: Enter and post consumption in the consumption journal manually. Forward: Automatically posts consumption according to the production order component lines when the first operation starts. Backward: Automatically calculates and posts consumption according to the production order component lines when the production order is finished. Pick + Forward / Pick + Backward: Variations with warehousing.

fields.name: Changed by User
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Specifies whether a leave message should be shown when you leave the Production Journal window.

fields.name: Originally Ordered No.
fields.tooltip: Contains the number of the item that was originally ordered.

fields.name: Originally Ordered Var. Code
fields.tooltip: Contains the code of the variant that was originally ordered.

fields.name: Out-of-Stock Substitution
fields.tooltip: Specifies that this the originally ordered item was out of stock.The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Item Category Code
fields.tooltip: Contains the item category code for the item on this line.

fields.name: Nonstock
fields.tooltip:
Specifies that the item on the line is a nonstock item (an item not normally kept in inventory).
The program copies the contents of the nonstock item field from the Nonstock field in the Sales Line table.
You cannot make changes to the contents of the this because the shipment has already been posted.

fields.name: Purchasing Code
fields.tooltip:
Contains the purchasing code for the item, which indicates the actions that were taken to purchase the item.
The program copies the purchasing code from the Purchasing Code field in the Sales Line table.
You cannot make changes to the contents of the this field because the shipment has already been posted.

fields.name: Item Reference No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a reference to the item number as defined by the item's barcode.

fields.name: Planned Delivery Date
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the planned date that the shipment will be delivered at the customer's address.
If the customer requests a delivery date, then the following occurs:

If the items are available for delivery, then the planned delivery date is the same as the requested delivery date.


If the items are not available for delivery, then the date that the items are available for delivery is calculated and entered in the Planned Delivery Date field.


If the customer does not request a delivery date, then the date that the items are available for delivery is calculated and entered in the Planned Delivery Date field.

fields.name: Order Date
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the order date, which is copied from the same field on the header when you create an order line and either enter a date or use the work date. You can change the order date on each line as necessary.
If you enter a requested receipt date on the order line, then the field is calculcated as:
Requested Receipt Date - Lead Time Calculation = Order Date
The order date then indicates the date on which you should order the item so that you receive it on time.

fields.name: Value Entry Type
fields.tooltip:
Contains the type of value described in this entry.
A value entry can be one of the following types:


Field


Description


Direct Cost

Cost that can be traced directly to the cost object.


Revaluation

A depreciation or appreciation of the current inventory value.


Rounding

Residuals caused by the valuation of inventory decrease.


Indirect Cost

Cost that is allocated without direct traceability to the cost object.


Variance

The difference between actual and standard costs that is only posted for items using the standard costing method.


The program automatically fills in this field.

fields.name: Item Charge No.
fields.tooltip:
Contains the item charge number of this journal line if you have entered an item charge on the line.
The program fills in the field automatically, and you can see the item charge number in the Value Entries window.

fields.name: Inventory Value (Calculated)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the calculated inventory value of the item at the specified posting date.

fields.name: Inventory Value (Revalued)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the new inventory value.

fields.name: Variance Type
fields.tooltip:
Contains the type of variance described in this entry.
If the type in the Entry Type field is Variance, the program fills in this field with the right type of variance, based on the document that created the value entry.
For example, if the variance is caused by a difference between the standard and actual cost of purchase, the program sets the type to Purchase.
The variance can be one of the following types:

<Blank>


Purchase


Material


Capacity


Subcontracted


Capacity Overhead


Manufacturing Overhead

fields.name: Inventory Value Per
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the calculation type used to sum up inventory value for this line.
The program uses the field internally and copies it from the Calculate Inventory Value batch job.

fields.name: Partial Revaluation
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if only part of the original inbound entry will be revalued. This value is set automatically by the program.
The program uses the field internally.

fields.name: Applies-from Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the outbound item ledger entry, whose cost is forwarded to the inbound item ledger entry.

fields.name: Invoice No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Unit Cost (Calculated)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the current unit cost of this item before revaluation.

fields.name: Unit Cost (Revalued)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the revalued unit cost of this item.

fields.name: Applied Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the revaluation of an average costed item.
It contains the total revaluation amount that must be applied to the value entries in order to reach the average cost.

fields.name: Update Standard Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the standard cost on the item will be updated when the revaluation is posted.

fields.name: Amount (ACY)
fields.tooltip: Contains the amount of the entry in the additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Correction
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if you want the journal line to be posted as a corrective entry.
This field is only used when the journal line acts as a revaluation journal line. For more information, see the Revaluing the Posted Items section in Correction of Incorrect Posted Documents.

fields.name: Adjustment
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the item journal is used to adjust costs through the Adjust Cost - Item Entries batch job.

fields.name: Applies-to Value Entry
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Invoice-to Source No.
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the journal type, which is either Work Center or Machine Center.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Cap. Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unit of measure code for the capacity usage.

fields.name: Qty. per Cap. Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: Specifies the value when you enter the capacity (Work Center/Machine Center) in the No. field if you have filled in the Unit of Measure Code field on the work center card and entered a quantity for the current operation in the Qty. per Unit of Measure field.

fields.name: Unit Cost Calculation
fields.tooltip:
Contains the value that will be used for the calculation of capacity.
If the capacity is calculated in units, the formula is Quantity = Output Quantity + Scrap Quantity.
If the capacity is calculated in time, the formula is Quantity = (Setup Time +) Run Time.
The setup time is only included if you have checkmarked the Cost Incl. Setup field in the manufacturing setup.

fields.name: Subcontracting
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if the purchase order entry on this journal line is from a subcontractor.
The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Serial No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the serial number of the item.

fields.name: Lot No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the lot number of the item.

fields.name: Warranty Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the warranty expiration date of the item.

fields.name: New Serial No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a new serial number that replaces the serial number in the Serial No. field when you post the item reclassification journal.

fields.name: New Lot No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a new lot number that replaces the lot number in the Lot No. field when you post the item reclassification journal.

fields.name: New Item Expiration Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies how to transfer the new expiration date from item tracking lines in the item reclassification journal to new item ledger entries.

fields.name: Item Expiration Date
fields.tooltip:
This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

The expiration date is the last date that the item on the line can be used. An expiration date is mainly used for perishable items, such as produce, that are not useable after a certain date.

fields.name: Package No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the package number of the item.

fields.name: Return Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Contains a code that explains why the item is returned.

fields.name: Price Calculation Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the method that will be used for price calculation in the item journal line.

fields.name: Warehouse Adjustment
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the item journal line is used to synchronize with warehouse entries through the Calculate Whse. Adjustment batch job.

fields.name: Phys Invt Counting Period Code
fields.tooltip: Contains the code of the counting period that indicates how often you want to count the item or stockkeeping unit in a physical inventory.

fields.name: Phys Invt Counting Period Type
fields.tooltip: Indicates whether the physical inventory counting period used on the line is assigned to the stockkeeping unit (SKU) or to the item.

fields.name: Overhead Rate
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the items indirect cost as an absolute amount. The amount is added to the items unit cost to reflect indirect cost or overhead, such as freight and warehouse handling that are associated with the purchase of the item.

Additional Information

The value of the Overhead Rate field on the item card is copied to item journal lines and is used to calculate the value in the Unit Cost field on the item journal line.
Note


The Overhead Rate field is related to the Indirect Cost % field where it defines the overhead as a percentage of the value in the Unit Amount field on the item journal line.

fields.name: Single-Level Material Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total material cost of all the items at the first level of the item's BOM.

fields.name: Single-Level Capacity Cost
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Single-Level Subcontrd. Cost
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Single-Level Cap. Ovhd Cost
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Single-Level Mfg. Ovhd Cost
fields.tooltip: There are a number of tables and fields that are not currently documented. There is no specific Help for these tables and fields.

fields.name: Rolled-up Material Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total material cost of all the items at all levels of the items BOM, added to the material cost of the item itself.

fields.name: Rolled-up Capacity Cost
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Rolled-up Subcontracted Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the current subcontracted cost of the item that results from subcontracting costs of its underlying components.
The value in this field is copied from the Rolled-up Subcontracted Cost field on the item card.

fields.name: Rolled-up Mfg. Ovhd Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the current manufacturing overhead cost of the item that results from manufacturing costs of its underlying components.
The value in this field is copied from the Rolled-up Mfg. Ovhd Cost field on the item card.

fields.name: Rolled-up Cap. Overhead Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the current capacity overhead cost of the item that results from capacity costs of its underlying components.
The value in this field is copied from the Rolled-up Capacity Cost field on the item card.
